title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A municipal accessibility remediation team. They need a TypeScript task intake and worker system with explicit delivery claims, AMQP topology, typed client lifecycle, heartbeat recovery, versioned declarations, direct routing, fair competing consumers, and durable effect evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Defended Work Distribution Service with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
project case — A project messaging team handles case 1805 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Project Outcome Evidence Contract, keep Outcome Evidence Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.
